image0.name: cosmological_observables_plot_1_20250926-105043.png
image0.caption: Impact of BRST-allowed quantum corrections on cosmological observables. The top panel illustrates the deviation of the gravitational wave speed (\(c_T^2 - 1\)) from unity as a function of redshift, showing that the GW170817 constraint severely limits the magnitude of the quantum correction coefficient \(\alpha_{H4}\). The bottom panel displays the scalar sound speed squared (\(c_s^2\)) versus redshift, demonstrating that these quantum corrections modify but preserve the stability of scalar perturbations by maintaining \(c_s^2 > 0\).
